sources.title: Apache Rifles (1964) One Man Vowed to Stop the Bloodshed! Western Movie by William Witney
sources.description:
Caught in the Crossfire of Passions and Greed!
Captain Jeff Stanton is tasked with returning the rebellious Apaches to their reservation. After capturing the chief’s son, he negotiates a fragile truce, promising to keep settlers off Indian lands. However, greedy gold miners and townsfolk sabotage the peace, leading to betrayal and violence. As Stanton’s views soften due to a romance with a part-Apache missionary, a new ruthless commander takes over, threatening to ignite a devastating conflict.

Original title: Apache Rifles (1964)  
Alternative titles: Apache Uprising, Rifles apaches, Rifles Apaches, Tamburi ad ovest, La Fureur des Apaches, Aufstand in Arizona, Apaçi Tüfekleri, Strzelby Apaczów


Director: William Witney
Writers: Charles Smith, Kenneth Gamet, Richard Schayer
Genre: Western
Main cast: 
Audie Murphy as Jeff Stanton
Linda Lawson as Dawn Gillis
Michael Dante as Red Hawk
L.Q. Jones as Mike Greer
Ken Lynch as Hodges
Joseph Vitale as Victorio


00:00 Full movie 
01:16 Captain Stanton's cavalry pursues Victorio's fleeing Apaches across Arizona Territory
19:04 Half-Comanche missionary Miss Gillis challenges Stanton's prejudices
31:16 Miners discover gold on Apache land, igniting violent tensions
42:21 Colonel Perry undermines Stanton's patrol strategy, leaving reservations vulnerable
47:56 Thompson's mysterious death sparks bloody conflict between miners and Apaches
1:21:26 Victorio falls in battle as Red Hawk assumes leadership and brokers peace with a conflicted Stanton

@CultCinemaClassics

sources.title: NEW 4K RESTORATION🍕 Apache Rifles (1964) AUDIE MURPHY
sources.description:
It's FRIDAY@PizzaFLIX
Director: William Witney
Stars: Audie Murphy, L.Q. Jones, Michael Dante, Linda Lawson
🍕 New HD 4K RESTORATION from the Original Negative🍕 
Experience a tense and action-filled Western directed by veteran filmmaker William Witney. Set in the rugged Arizona Territory, this 1964 CinemaScope feature stars Audie Murphy as Capt. Jeff Stanton, a tough but principled Army officer, assigned to suppress a growing Apache uprising. As conflict escalates, Stanton must confront not only the violence on the frontier but also his own changing beliefs about justice and peace.

This fast-paced Western also stars Michael Dante as the proud Apache leader, and Linda Lawson as the strong-willed missionary’s daughter caught in the crossfire. Adding grit to the cast is L.Q. Jones—later a frequent face in Sam Peckinpah’s legendary posse—bringing classic frontier toughness to the screen.

Are you a PizzaFLIX Fanatic? Join our exclusive Fan Club for dedicated Classic movie lovers: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCE34fwgW7kWr7tc7YiOWtRw/join

A standout entry in the final era of traditional Hollywood Westerns, this production delivers compelling performances and cinematic style. Whether you’re a fan of Audie Murphy’s filmography or a student of 1960s Westerns, this restored presentation brings the frontier back to life.

#ApacheRifles #AudieMurphy #WesternMovies #WilliamWitney #ClassicWesterns #MichaelDante #LindaLawson #CinemaScope #LQJones

metadata.Plot: In the Arizona Territory in 1879, Captain Jeff Stanton takes command of a cavalry unit tasked with returning the Apaches to their reservation. The Mescaleros Apaches, led by chief Victorio, have left the reservation and openly attacked farmers,gold miners and settlers who trespassed on their lands. During a skirmish, Captain Stanton captures Red Hawk, the son of the chief, and uses him to bargain for a truce.Stanton promises to chief Victorio to see that gold miners and settlers are kept off Indian lands.However, some greedy townsfolk and disfranchised gold miners plot to break the uneasy truce.They murder the Indian agent and blame the Apaches.They complain to Washington D.C. about Captain Stanton's negotiated truce.Stanton is replaced by Colonel Perry who intends to use a heavy hand in subduing the Apaches.The consequence of this strategy change proves disastrous.
metadata.Writer: Charles Smith, Kenneth Gamet, Richard Schayer
metadata.Actors: Audie Murphy, Michael Dante, Linda Lawson
